title: Hospital Admission Checklist
summary: For a planned or sudden hospital stay — what to bring and confirm on the way in.

tasks.title: Grab the go bag
tasks.explanation: Phone charger, glasses/hearing aids, a change of clothes.
tasks.notes: Ideally pre-packed in advance — see "Hospital Admission: What to Bring" article.

tasks.title: Bring the current medication list
tasks.explanation: One of the single most useful things to hand the admitting team.

tasks.title: Bring insurance card and photo ID
Confirm the reason for admission in your own words
tasks.explanation: Make sure it matches what you understood from the referring doctor or ER.

tasks.title: Identify the main point of contact for the care team
tasks.explanation: One family member the team should call with updates.

tasks.title: Ask when daily rounds typically happen
tasks.explanation: This is your best window for questions each day.

tasks.title: Keep a running question list
tasks.explanation: Add questions as they come up rather than trying to remember them.

tasks.title: Ask daily: "what's the plan for today?"
tasks.explanation: More useful than a general "how are they doing."

tasks.title: Watch for and mention any sudden confusion
tasks.explanation: Hospital delirium is common and often treatable — worth flagging right away.
